examples.title: Basic Friend Class Example
examples.description: Simple program demonstrating friend class accessing private members
examples.explanation: Class Rectangle is declared as friend of Box, allowing it to access Box's private members width and height directly. This demonstrates how friend classes can access private data.

examples.title: Demonstrating One-Way Friendship
examples.description: Program showing that friendship is one-way
examples.explanation: This demonstrates one-way friendship. B can access A's private data because A declared B as friend. However, A cannot access B's private data unless B also declares A as friend.

examples.title: Demonstrating Non-Inheritance of Friendship
examples.description: Program showing friendship is not inherited by derived classes
examples.explanation: Shows that friendship is not inherited. Even though B is a friend of A, DerivedB (inherited from B) cannot access A's private members.

examples.title: BankAccount and Transaction with Friend Class
examples.description: Real-world example demonstrating appropriate use of friend class
examples.explanation: This demonstrates an appropriate use of friend class. Transaction class needs direct access to BankAccount's private balance to perform operations efficiently. Without friend, we'd need public getters/setters which expose balance to everyone.

examples.title: Friend Class in Different Sections
examples.description: Shows that friend declaration can be placed in any access specifier section
examples.explanation: This demonstrates that the location of friend declaration (public, protected, or private) does not affect its functionality. All three work the same way.
